$comment: Machine-readable repository index for AI agents. Answer location questions from HERE before opening source. Keep synchronized with docs/ARCHITECTURE.md in the same commit. Human entry point: agents.md.

project.name: ViewLab (XR ViewLab)
project.what: OpenXR implicit API layer (FOV/resolution crop + D3D11 visor mask drawn into game eye textures) + WPF settings app. Ships as MSI.

canonicalDocs.updatePolicy: Behavioral change => update STATE.md (+CHANGELOG.md if user-visible) in the SAME commit; key change => docs/CONFIG.md; permanent decision => docs/DECISIONS.md; regression => docs/REGRESSIONS.md; subsystem shape change => docs/ARCHITECTURE.md + repo-index.json. A commit with none of these requires explicit justification in the commit message (docs-only, comment-only, or build-artifact-only).

expensive.rule: grep the symbol from subsystems below; never read end-to-end
grep the symbol/key constant; never read end-to-end

renderCrop.what: Core perf feature: crops FOV tangents + recommended render size the game sees
invariants: crop changes FOV tangents only and never mutates XrView pose/orientation
recommended resolution follows effective tangent coverage
visual-mask-only modes do not reduce recommended resolution

visorMask.what: Black visor and ViewLab features use one exclusive frame-capability plan: direct during allocation, then the proven ordered stereo projection when later layers can cover it
invariants: preview and native geometry formula-identical (D5)
y-flip ONLY in ApexYFromConfigNdc
nose divot anchored to NDC y0, band-clamped
mask_nose_spread_x mirrors outward and zero preserves prior geometry
FreeLibrary after all shader blob use (R1)
Curve 0 emits an exact rectangular aperture
mask_size scales only the aperture; it never changes crop/FOV/recommended resolution
plain projection uses direct rendering
direct owns ordered allocation and stops when the ordered carrier is ready
all normal features transfer and fall back together
a layered projection uses the proven two-slice transparent projection appended after application layers
API submission success is not visibility proof
backend failure never classifies the game as unsupported

stencilPipeline.what: 'Stencil outer edges only' checkbox: filters runtime FOV-stencil mesh + switches visor bean/arch + gates partner boundary
invariants: filter runs even when visor active
indivisible one-triangle runtime masks pass through unchanged
filtering never turns a non-empty runtime mesh into an empty mesh
DLL reads stencil_outer_edges_only (legacy fallback outer_edge_visibility_mask_only)
crop_outer_edges_only selects outer-only versus full horizontal FOV crop
game restart required to re-query mask

configPlumbing.what: UI writes ini + per-app registry; DLL reads a stable snapshot at game startup
invariants: every key must exist on BOTH sides (R3)
bundled xr-viewlab.ini must carry every product key (R6/R9)
MSI upgrades preserve the live LOCALAPPDATA ini and per-app HKCU profiles
global visor tuning is always live via the generation-stamped mapping and never overrides a per-app profile
Use global visor settings clears only the visor override; only Use Global Values clears profile_enabled
profile Save reloads the app list from registry
startup publishes once after settings hydration
live shared-memory reconnect is bounded to once per second while the UI is closed
encodings: millis v*1000, signed (v+1)*1000

previewCanvas.what: WPF visor preview with draggable mask pins plus live move/scale handles and labelled placement indicators for every enabled common overlay; THE reference spec for native visor geometry
invariants: the preview is a trustworthy runtime calibration mirror with four layers: full frame, periphery guide, post-crop coverage and final visor
the centred Quest 3 outer box has fixed 55:48 aspect
crop percentages map directly once: horizontal 0.8 is 80% width, symmetric vertical 0.15 is 15% height, and vertical 1.0 is the exact outer-frame height
preview_per_eye_frames independently toggles one combined frame or two overlapping 2064:2208 per-eye rectangles
preview_circle_guides toggles one binocular oval or two overlapping circles over the same 85%-wide/90%-high boundary
preview_ipd_mm defaults to 67.0 and direct typing, keyboard steps and visible buttons share one 0.1 mm adjustment path
all guide preferences are UI-only and cannot change crop, visor, overlays or runtime
circle guides use square bounds and equal radii
preview contains no projection-degree/tangent reinterpretation
preview labels and handles retain screen-space size under zoom
reference footprints store no baked scale
Scale is applied exactly once to both axes and bounds fitting uses one aspect-preserving factor
overlay preview layout and hit-testing use the full H/V 1.00 area rather than crop
ordinary node move/scale edits persist and publish through the shared overlay settings path
preview resize pins consume the same slider minimum and maximum used by direct controls
active visor geometry is red and disabled geometry is faint grey
preview editing never owns a second settings store
edge-only cues remain read-only until they have a shared placement contract
labelled footprints are not called pixel-identical replicas until native layout/content data is available

calibrationDiagnostics.what: Ten optional diagnostic patterns drawn into submitted D3D11 eye textures after the visor, with literal-pixel tools separated from tangent-space perceptual geometry
invariants: all ten keys default off
patterns draw at release time after visor
temporal patterns advance from submitted projection frames, never eye-release count
one-pixel patterns must be capture-checked at 100% zoom
pixel measurement tools use literal submitted-texture pixels and the complete eye rectangle rather than crop-overlap scaling
the 64px grid is anchored at shared tangent zero but keeps exact 64px spacing
radial circles and spokes are constructed in shared tangent space and projected per eye

performanceHud.what: Optional stereo visor-native performance HUD drawn directly into both submitted D3D11 projection eye textures, anchored in the shared tangent-space overlap so it fuses cleanly
invariants: hardware PDH/DXGI/system/ICMP reads occur only on the bounded telemetry worker
network widgets describe the configured probe path and never claim hidden game-server telemetry
network probing is at most one echo per second and OFF requires three consecutive misses
rendering consumes TryGetSnapshot without waiting and both eyes reuse one immutable draw snapshot
enabled widgets pack in persisted order into one proportional row
HUD scale changes complete widget geometry and gaps together
every symbol uses one time-based sustained alarm state machine whose recovery hold cannot refresh itself
GPU defaults to 90 percent warning and 98 percent critical
SYS is remaining headroom from the strongest valid pressure, never an average or OpenXR duration
APP means begin-return to end-entry wall time divided by cadence-aware budget
VR cadence comes from wait-to-wait QPC intervals relative to current predictedDisplayPeriod
every graph mode has a compatible useful channel
trace modes are off, always, or alarm-only; alarm-only responds only to represented channels then uses one hold/fade
live history is fixed at 600 samples; the post-session trace is a bounded one-hour ring checkpointed and durably flushed once per second by the telemetry worker without relying on shutdown callbacks
marker binds are exact QPC events in the real trace and never generic history
GPU selects the D3D11 render adapter and never sums unrelated engines
unavailable telemetry is never fabricated
Topmost and crop/projection paths are independent

viewLabBridge.what: Internal native boundary for staged legacy VR translation and central frame-capability presentation policy
invariants: no application-name route policy
all ViewLab features share one presentation plan
external translation remains active until internal ABI parity
normal UI exposes no transport names
runtime and submitted-frame facts select fallbacks
texture bounds map against current texture dimensions

completedSessionGraph.what: Interpretable reader and desktop graph for durable OpenXR session traces with schema 1 compatibility and additive schema 2 GPU alarm evidence
invariants: schema 1 traces remain readable
schema 2 additively anchors UTC and persists already-collected GPU and alarm state
min-max pixel buckets preserve spikes
axes and hover values state units
OpenXR-derived cadence misses are estimates and never claimed as measured dropped presentations

diagMonMigration.what: View Lab-native generic performance capture, portable session history, deterministic comparable baselines and bounded AI evidence export migrated from useful DiagMon experiments
invariants: ViewLab is the only shipping product and sole owner of collectors
legacy DiagMon folders scripts and runtime are never product dependencies
standard capture never starts WPR
target exit and Trace deadline automatically run the same serialised finaliser as manual stop
the pinned PresentMon collector streams stdout into a ViewLab-owned durably flushed CSV and is stopped by its unique ETW session name
DiagMon fixtures use a temporary store and never the production LocalAppData index
Detailed module evidence is sampled while the target is alive
collector success is based on evidence rather than orchestrator exit
AllowsTearing is never interpreted as a dropped frame
invalid experimental stress-test and incomplete sessions never enter calculated baselines
all historical inputs and selection reasons are recorded
raw evidence is never silently deleted
only collector processes owned by the active session are stopped

clockWidget.what: Live configurable one/two-lane stereo clock card with 12/24-hour time, elapsed session time and theme presets
invariants: elapsed time uses monotonic uptime and never wall-clock subtraction
the epoch is successful xrCreateSession and resets at xrDestroySession
timer visibility is independent and collapses the card to one lane
theme format placement scale opacity and bind publish live
the widget is independent of notifications and performance alarms
both eyes use the common shared-tangent Direct/Topmost overlay path

stickyNoteWidget.what: Bounded collection of independently configured square native visor notes with a shared visibility bind
invariants: at most eight notes
each input is capped at 120 characters and native output at four lines
each note owns enable theme position scale and opacity
legacy single-note settings migrate to slot zero
visibility bind is rising-edge triggered
notes never enter notification shared memory
both eyes use the common shared-tangent Direct/Topmost overlay path

obsRecordingIndicator.what: Optional subtle native recording cue driven by authenticated real OBS WebSocket recording state
invariants: OBS process presence is never recording evidence
broker uses WebSocket v5 GetRecordStatus
native reads a generation-stable dedicated mapping
capture exclusion is unverified and must not be claimed
cue defaults off

musicTrackChange.what: Opt-in brief title, artist and artwork card emitted only when the Windows Now Playing track changes
invariants: opt-in and default off
unchanged title plus artist never replays a card
pause seek and volume changes do not emit
artwork is optional
no transport controls or permanent widget
start and stop use the same named event handler

visorOverlays.what: Stereo visor overlays sharing HUD tangent-space anchoring: boundary flash, static CS crosshair with matching previews, real Windows notifications from an independent packaged broker, and generic events from an optional iRacing SDK provider. Direct rendering separates complete projection context from per-swapchain texture ownership.
invariants: fused overlays start from one frame-level target in complete shared binocular tangent space and project independently into each asymmetric eye; ProjectionFrameContext is independent of swapchain texture ownership and preserves all primary-stereo sub-image packings
ordinary overlay anchors and full-widget clamp/size bounds use FullLens so crop clips without relocation or deformation
fixed angular sizes use per-eye pixels-per-tangent, never cropped dimensions
crosshair shared tangent is 0,0 with zero unintended angular disparity
Render Area and Lens Pinned clamp in shared tangent space, never independently normalized eye pixels
boundary flash is a fully inset per-eye inner outline
projection-only applications remain on direct rendering
a distinct application compositor layer latches topmost demand
topmost allocation is attempted at most once per session and any failure latches direct fallback
the transition frame prepares topmost without submitting it so no direct/topmost duplicate appears
literal-pixel calibration always draws into the submitted game texture
notification animation is evaluated from lifecycle timestamps at native frame cadence with alpha applied once
flat overlay colours use kOverlayPS constant colour, never the VDXR-broken interpolant
combined visor preview shows one fused reference crosshair
notification collection lives in the signed medium-integrity broker and survives settings exit
presentation tests use temporary mapping flags and never enable production features
racing events never enable or persist global Windows notifications
iRacing polling is one cancellation-bound worker; only advancing valid SDK ticks publish generic events; stale/inactive/disconnect clears cues
native renderer has no iRacing SDK dependency
alarm-only HUD state must not suppress notifications

uiShell.what: Main settings window: app table, sliders, update check, layouts

reshadeRemote.what: Advanced popout controlling bundled ReShade OpenXR payload via shared memory
invariants: shared-memory XRControlBlock layout frozen
commands edge-triggered
bundled ReShade64.dll must hash-match the canonical source build

buildPackaging.what: Version bump + WPF publish + native x64/Win32 + WiX MSI + layer registration
invariants: build.ps1 auto-bumps version (manual builds must bump AssemblyInfo.cs + Product.wxs by hand)
MSI packages a fresh-install xr-viewlab.ini template and preserves live user settings on upgrade
Start Menu component key path is HKLM for per-machine install
publish path is derived from xr-viewlab.csproj TargetFramework and must match WiX SettingsApp source
finished MSI is administratively extracted and its app version plus WPF/native hashes, pinned PresentMon hash and notice, and compiled overlay markers must match fresh outputs
publish output copies freshly-built native DLLs after MSBuild
installer registers x64 + WOW6432Node layer manifests

layerBootstrap.what: OpenXR loader negotiation and hook installation

activeWork.milestone: visor feature rewrite in 4 passes; see STATE.md 'Active milestone' for live status
